title: Single cell passaging of hPSC

steps: Transfer hPSC suspension to a 15 or 50 mL conical tube. Several wells or vessels can be combined for centrifugation step.
Add enzymatic dissociation reagent, refer to Table 1 for recommended working volumes.
Add hPSC media supplemented with survival factors to the wells containing dissociation reagent, refer to Table 1 for recommended harvesting volumes.

Incubate the vessel at 37 °C for 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 . Incubation time depends on reagent used, coating and hPSC line attachment properties. Therefore, monitoring under microscope every 2-3 min until hPSC detachment observation is recommended. If necessary, gently tap culture vessel from the side to detach hPSC.

Pipette up and down several times to dislodge the cells from the culture surface. Pipette selection for this step depends on total hPSC suspension volume. The smaller the tip of the pipette the better cells will be singularized.
Aspirate and discard supernatant without disrupting the cell pellet.
Resuspend cell pellet in hPSC media supplemented with survival factors. Resuspension volume will depend on pellet size in a range of 1-10 mL.
Perform hPSC counting using viability staining such as Trypan Blue 0.4%. Counting can be performed manually using e.g. Neubauer Chamber or automated cell counting devices.

Adjust hPSC suspension volume according to desired number of cells for seeding, refer to Table 1 for recommended final media volumes. As example, for hPSC single cell passaging for hPSC maintenance in 6 well-plates, we recommend a seeding density between 200,000-400,000 cells per well. Seeding densities have to be tested and adjusted per hPSC line for optimal passaging.
Aspirate coating solution from the destination vessel.
Pipette cell suspension into the coated recipient wells according to desired hPSC seeding density, refer to Table 1 for recommended final media volumes.
Gently move destination vessels with hPSC in suspension in cross shape to distribute them evenly.
Incubate the vessels at 37 °C and at 5 % volume CO 2 .

After 24:00:00 , perform full media change with respective hPSC maintenance media without survival factors. For further hPSC culture refer to protocol Maintenance of hPSC . hPSC display different morphology when cultured in medium containing survival factors. This morphology will change after media is replaced with medium without survival factors. For detailed morphology, refer to protocol Reference pictures of hPSC cultured in defined conditions

Prepare coated tissue culture vessels and culture media according to hPSC line requirements and desired format ( Table 1 ). Refer to protocols: Coating of tissue Culture Vessels for hPSC and Maintenance of hPSC . %s %s
Equilibrate prepared destination vessel at 37 °C until usage.
Aspirate and discard medium from the selected source vessel.

Prepare required volume of the reagents and media according to the Table 1. Equilibrate the media to Room temperature . Culture media aliquot to be used can be warmed up at 37°C for 00:15:00 . However, to preserve recombinant proteins activity Room temperature equilibration is recommended.
